title: ElevenLabs raises $500m funding round at an $11bn valuation - Music Ally
text:
We’ve been writing about AI company ElevenLabs increasingly regularly in the past year, including its first licensing deals
with Kobalt and Merlin
and its recent
humans-meet-AI album featuring Liza Minnelli and Art Garfunkel
.
Now there’s some news on the corporate front. ElevenLabs has raised a $500m Series-D funding round that values the company at $11bn
according to the Wall Street Journal
, which noted that this is triple its valuation just a year ago.
Its interview with CEO Mati Staniszewski revealed that ElevenLabs generated annual recurring revenue of $330m last year, with hopes to double that total this year.
ElevenLabs’ music journey began last July via
a partnership with sync platform SourceAudio
, which provided millions of pre-cleared songs to train the company’s models.
That helped it to launch its Eleven Music GenAI model in August, but it said then that this would be followed by an ‘Eleven Music Pro’ model trained on music licensed from Merlin and Kobalt – and any other rightsholder deals.
In October, ElevenLabs was
listed by the Financial Times
as one of the AI companies who’d been in talks with Universal Music Group and Warner Music Group over possible deals. $500m of new funding may well presage announcements on that front.
Stuart Dredge
Music Ally's Head of Insight
More by Stuart Dredge
